rows.notes: Top-of-page title. Very clear printed/inked script for the page heading; no monetary amounts present.
Section heading directly under the page title, clearly underlined; no amounts present.

Amount reads as 5715 pounds, 4 shillings, 6 pence. Individual digits are legible and align with the pounds/shillings/pence ruling; value also confirmed by subtraction from the combined cash total (see row 5).
Reads clearly as 236 pounds, 15 shillings, 10 pence. Alignment with the three vertical ruling lines is unambiguous; used with row 3 to confirm combined cash total.
Subtotal line for cash/library (no text label, only a rule and a figure). Arithmetic check: 5715 4s 6d + 236 15s 10d = 5952 0s 4d (carries handled). Column alignment for the total is clear.
Single round figure '3000' clearly written in the pounds column; no shillings or pence indicated. Alignment is unambiguous.
Clearly reads 6511 pounds, 14 shillings, 2 pence. Vertical column boundaries are clear and values are within valid ranges.
Subtotal (Furniture + Investments) shown to the right. Arithmetic check: 3000 + 6511 14s 2d = 9511 14s 2d. Line and figures are well aligned and clear.
Section heading (underlined). No monetary amount on the heading line itself.
Shows '134' in the pounds column with no shillings/pence indicated (written as '134.'). Alignment with ruling lines is clear.
Reads as 224 pounds, 15 shillings, 3 pence; characters are legible and column alignment is clear.
Clearly written as 53 pounds, 13 shillings, 4 pence; ink and alignment are clear.
Reads '1 15 .' (1 pound 15 shillings). Pence column blank. Alignment with shillings column is clear.
Shown as '70.' in the pounds column; shillings/pence blank. Clear and unambiguous.
Subtotal for the 'Due to the College' group shown on the same ruled line to the right. Arithmetic and alignment clear: sum matches the individual items above.
Amount reads clearly as 18 pounds, 10 shillings, 11 pence. The descriptive text is less certain — the first word looks like 'How' or possibly 'Haw'/'Howe'; chosen as 'How' with moderate confidence.
Reads 16 pounds, 14 shillings (no pence). The surname 'Mitchell' is legible; amount aligns well with columns.

Amount 19 pounds, 12 shillings, 7 pence is clear. The fund name is in cursive and somewhat uncertain; appears as 'Loscombe Richards Fund' (the first word might be 'Loscombe'/'Loxcombe'/'Coscombe'); chosen reading reflects the most likely letters but is given moderate confidence.
Subtotal for the small funds group. Clear alignment and arithmetic: 18 10 11 + 16 14 0 + 19 12 7 = 54 17 6 (checked with column addition and carry).

rows.description: I certify that the accounts of the College are duly
rows.notes: First line of the attestation paragraph. Handwriting is legible though slightly slanted; no monetary amounts present.

rows.description: kept in proper books of account & that the Abstracts
rows.notes: Continuation of the attestation paragraph; text readable, no amounts.

rows.description: and Balance Sheet contain a full & true account
rows.notes: Third line of attestation paragraph; wording clear though slightly compressed; no amounts present.

rows.description: of the financial condition of the College.
rows.notes: Final line of the attestation paragraph; sentence completion clearly legible.
Date is clearly written 'March 8th 1901' and well legible.

Signature line — the letters are highly stylized. Reads approximately as 'W.C. Tacktad F.C.O.' but the surname and post-nominal letters are uncertain (signature form). Low confidence because of stylized handwriting.

Second signature block is cursive and difficult to read. Interpreted as 'Jackson Ridley Browning' with an illegible suffix; included as header because no monetary amount is present. Very low confidence due to heavy stylization and overlap with flourishes.

Final grand total at bottom right of page, clearly written as '£16002 15 7'. Alignment with the right-hand columns and clear digits give high confidence. This total also reconciles with the sum of the grouped subtotals (checked).
